channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Orlando Pirates mourn the passing of long-time supporter Charles 'Makhalanjalo' Fantisi
item.description: Orlando Pirates are in mourning following the passing of one of their most devoted supporters, Charles 'Makhalanjalo' Fantisi. Fantisi passed away on Friday, 8 March 2026, at a hospital in Carletonvil...

item.title: Rising Cost of Politics Threatens Inclusive Democracy in Nigeria
item.description: For Nigeria, di wahala wey dey make politics expensive don dey worry plenty people. From high party nomination fees, campaign logistics, media exposure, litigation costs to new regulatory charges like...

item.title: Latino Voters Boost James Talarico in Texas Democratic Senate Primary
item.description: In the Texas Democratic Senate primary held on March 3, 2026, Latino voters in several majority-Latino counties showed unprecedented turnout for Democratic candidates, surpassing even the votes cast f...

item.title: Kiambu Woman Goes Missing While Delivering Goods in Nairobi, Leaves Behind Infant
item.description: A family in Githunguri, Kiambu County is grappling with worry and distress after Maureen Nyambura Gathungu, a mother of a six-month-old baby, disappeared while delivering goods in Nairobi on March 6. ...

item.title: Kisan Trust Celebrates Courage and Resilience of Women on International Women’s Day
item.description: On the occasion of International Women’s Day 2026, Kisan Trust held the Aparajita Samman Samaroh, a programme dedicated to celebrating the courage, resilience, and achievements of women from diverse p...

item.title: Oil Prices Jump Over $100 per Barrel Amid Rising Tensions in Iran
item.description: Oil prices have risen above $100 per barrel for the first time since Russia’s 2022 invasion of Ukraine, driven by escalating conflict in Iran. US crude futures increased by 14.7%, while Brent, the glo...

item.title: Inside the Struggling Sameri Police Post in Kitengela
item.description: In Kitengela, Kajiado County, the Sameri Police Post is still facing serious challenges nearly two years after officers were finally deployed. The facility is poorly maintained, with no electricity fo...

item.title: Toronto Maple Leafs face challenges in securing a top draft pick
item.description: The Toronto Maple Leafs are facing a difficult situation as they attempt to preserve their 2026 first-round draft pick, currently top-five protected for Boston. With the team underperforming, discussi...

item.title: Parliament to investigate Migori public land lease to firm linked to Obado family
item.description: Members of Parliament have moved to scrutinise a controversial land allocation in Migori County involving a firm associated with the family of former governor Okoth Obado. Lawmakers are concerned that...

item.title: How to make a Cheddar dip and charcuterie grazing board
item.description: This article provides a recipe and serving guide for a Mainland Cheddar dip paired with a charcuterie grazing board. The focus is on creating a relaxed, communal food experience where guests can help ...

item.title: Lagos State Government Clarifies Plans for Monthly Environmental Sanitation
item.description: The Lagos State Government don clarify say di online report wey talk say dem don resume monthly environmental sanitation exercise no true. Mr Tokunbo Wahab, di Commissioner for Environment and Water R...

item.title: Police Report 42 Deaths from Floods Across Kenya
item.description: Heavy rains have pounded several parts of Kenya, triggering devastating floods that have killed 42 people nationwide since Friday, according to police reports. Nairobi has been hardest hit, with 26 de...

item.title: Reno Omokri Defends Tinubu Over Unrealistic Power Supply Promise
item.description: For real now, the issue of power supply for Naija don dey hot lately. For March 5, 2026, Reno Omokri, wey once dey media team for President Goodluck Jonathan, defend President Bola Ahmed Tinubu for Ch...

item.title: Deputy Minister Gungubele Launches African Digital Transformation Centre in Pretoria
item.description: This afternoon, Deputy Minister of Communications and Digital Technologies, Mondli Gungubele, will officially launch the African Digital Transformation Centre (ADTC) at The Innovation Hub in Pretoria....

item.title: Fire at Vape Shop Forces Closure of Glasgow Central Station
item.description: A fire at a vape shop in Union Street has led to the closure of Glasgow Central Station, Scotland's busiest railway station, until further notice. The incident, which began on Sunday afternoon, saw mo...

item.title: ASX expected to drop as oil prices hit four-year highs amid Middle East tensions
item.description: The Australian sharemarket is set to open lower following significant losses on Wall Street, as oil prices soar past US$100 per barrel for the first time in four years due to ongoing conflict in Iran....

item.title: Indian Citizen Killed in Saudi Arabia Amid West Asia Conflict; 12 Injured in Residential Area Strike
item.description: An Indian national was tragically killed in Saudi Arabia on Sunday when a military projectile struck a residential area in Al-Kharj city. Alongside the Indian citizen, a Bangladeshi national also lost...

item.title: DMW and Global Anti-Scam Alliance Sign MOU to Enhance Protection for Overseas Filipino Workers Against Online Scams
item.description: The Department of Migrant Workers (DMW) has entered into a formal partnership with the Global Anti-Scam Alliance (GASA) through a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) to combat the rising threat of sophi...

item.title: How Investors Can Manage Portfolios Amid Ongoing Middle East Conflict
item.description: The recent outbreak of conflict in the Middle East has unsettled global markets, though initial reactions were muted. The TSX saw a slight rise, while U.S. markets experienced minor declines. Escalati...

item.title: US Justice Department Releases Previously Withheld FBI Interviews Alleging Trump Sexual Assault of Minor in Epstein Case
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice recently published additional documents from its files on convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, including FBI interview summaries (known as 302 reports) that had been ...
